events.event_summary: Micron (MU) is down 13.2% on its earnings day, suggesting a disappointing print or guidance for the memory sector.
primary_drivers: Memory cycle read-through (DRAM/NAND pricing and demand)
Capex pullback signal for memory equipment vendors

losers.rationale: Pure-play NAND/HDD peer; Micron weak guide directly signals softer NAND pricing and demand into WDC's next quarter.
Storage peer levered to same cloud/enterprise data storage demand cycle MU just flagged as weakening.
Memory is ~half of Lam's WFE revenue; a MU guide-down typically precedes memory capex cuts at MU/Samsung/SK Hynix.
Large memory equipment exposure; memory capex digestion read-through hits AMAT's deposition/etch orders.
Process control tools for memory fabs; capex slowdown at memory makers pressures order book.
HBM-levered metrology play; if MU's HBM commentary disappointed, ONTO's AI-memory thesis takes a hit.

predictions.rationale: Large memory equipment exposure; memory capex digestion read-through hits AMAT's deposition/etch orders.
